channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: This 10-second add-on turns any bicycle into a 90 km-range e-bike
item.dc:creator: Penghui LI
item.description: During a recent offline tech event in China, one of our colleagues had the chance to try a small device that promises something unusually practical: turning a regular bicycle into an electric-assist bike in under ten seconds. The product is called Kamingo, built by a team of former Huawei and BYD engineers, and it aims […]
item.content:encoded:
During a recent offline tech event in China, one of our colleagues had the chance to try a small device that promises something unusually practical: turning a regular bicycle into an electric-assist bike in under ten seconds. The product is called Kamingo, built by a team of former Huawei and BYD engineers, and it aims to offer a lightweight, reversible alternative to buying a full e-bike.

A tiny kit with big ambitions

Kamingo weighs just 2.3 kilograms and consists of three pieces: a friction-drive motor that presses onto the rear tire, a bottle-shaped battery pack that fits into a normal bottle cage, and a handlebar controller. First-time installation takes about three minutes, but afterward the device can be attached or removed in less than ten seconds, without tools.

Once installed, Kamingo offers three modes:

  • Standby, which lets the bike ride normally with the motor lifted off the tire
  • Assist, which provides pedal-based power support
  • Cruise, which drives the bike electrically without pedaling

The battery, made with 21700 EV-grade cells, supports up to 90 km (55 miles) of range in assist mode and doubles as a USB-C power bank. During our brief test ride, the motor remained surprisingly quiet — rated at ≤ 56 dB, quieter than typical conversation. The system uses Pressure Adaptive Technology to adjust the motor’s contact force based on load and surface conditions, reducing slipping and improving efficiency.

Both the motor and battery carry IP66 dust and water protection, and safety features include tilt-cutoff (power stops if the bike tips) and foreign-object detection. The controller itself is also quick-release, making it easier to lock the bike when parking.

A clever idea, with natural limitations

Like all friction-drive systems, Kamingo works best with smoother tires. Bikes with aggressive mountain-bike treads or certain fender setups may need adjustments for ideal performance. And while the peak power is rated at 750 W, continuous torque and long-hill performance will still depend heavily on rider tests and real-world conditions.

Still, for riders who don’t want to buy and maintain a full e-bike, the idea of a reversible, on-demand electric assist is compelling.

Price and who it’s for

Kamingo launched on Kickstarter with early-bird pricing starting at US$349 (about RMB 2,480) — far below the price of most complete e-bikes.

It’s aimed at riders who:

  • commute but don’t want a heavy e-bike
  • ride casually and want occasional assistance
  • prefer keeping their existing bicycle but want a modular upgrade path

A companion mobile app allows users to lock the motor, view ride data, access maintenance guides, and join an online community of riders.

The bottom line

Kamingo isn’t trying to replace high-end e-bikes or compete on sheer power. Instead, it delivers something simpler and more flexible: an instant electric upgrade that preserves the feel of a normal bicycle.

If long-term performance proves reliable and compatibility issues remain manageable, this ten-second add-on could carve out a new category in e-mobility — one where electrifying a bike is as quick as clipping on an accessory.

item.title: Alibaba CEO says AI bubble unlikely in next three years
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Alibaba CEO Eddie Wu said an artificial intelligence bubble is unlikely to emerge in the next three years, as AI resources will remain in short supply. His comments followed Alibaba’s fiscal Q2 2026 results, where the company reported about RMB 247.8 billion (USD 34.6 billion) in revenue, up 15 percent after excluding divested businesses. Wu […]
item.content:encoded:
Alibaba CEO Eddie Wu said an artificial intelligence bubble is unlikely to emerge in the next three years, as AI resources will remain in short supply. His comments followed Alibaba’s fiscal Q2 2026 results, where the company reported about RMB 247.8 billion (USD 34.6 billion) in revenue, up 15 percent after excluding divested businesses. Wu said Alibaba is in an investment phase building AI infrastructure and consumer platforms, and noted strong demand for cloud-based AI products.

Alibaba said it will continue investing in AI capabilities and may expand its previously announced RMB 380 billion (USD 53 billion) three-year investment plan, adding that the earlier amount may now be considered small given customer demand. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: BMW considers range-extender versions as Chinese rivals reshape demand
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: BMW is evaluating range-extender versions of some models as demand for the technology grows in China, Bloomberg reported. The plan is under internal discussion and focuses on large vehicles such as the X5 and 7-Series. Chinese brands including Aito and Li Auto have popularized range extenders in the world’s biggest car market, which produced 31.28 […]
item.content:encoded: BMW is evaluating range-extender versions of some models as demand for the technology grows in China, Bloomberg reported. The plan is under internal discussion and focuses on large vehicles such as the X5 and 7-Series. Chinese brands including Aito and Li Auto have popularized range extenders in the world’s biggest car market, which produced 31.28 million vehicles and sold 31.44 million in 2024, where BMW’s sales have come under pressure. The technology is also gaining traction in the US, where customers favor large SUVs and long-distance travel. BMW already produces small engines and gearboxes in-house, which could reduce costs if the project moves forward. [Bloomberg]

item.title: Li Auto to launch first AI smart glasses, expanding multi-device ecosystem
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Li Xiang, founder of Li Auto, said on Tuesday the company will soon launch its first pair of AI smart glasses, calling it “Li Auto’s best AI accessory.” He said the device will be deeply integrated with in-car use cases, supporting AR navigation, fatigue monitoring and other functions that work in tandem with the company’s […]
item.content:encoded: Li Xiang, founder of Li Auto, said on Tuesday the company will soon launch its first pair of AI smart glasses, calling it “Li Auto’s best AI accessory.” He said the device will be deeply integrated with in-car use cases, supporting AR navigation, fatigue monitoring and other functions that work in tandem with the company’s intelligent cockpit system. Li Auto is also planning an AI smart speaker ecosystem as it builds a multi-terminal matrix combining glasses + speaker + vehicle system. The company has set up a “wearable robot” unit to lead development, with the first product expected to feature a lightweight design aimed at enhancing travel-related experiences. [Li Xiang’s bilibili, in Chinese]

item.title: Huawei launches first companion chat robot Smart Hanhan, priced at about $55
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Huawei on Tuesday unveiled its first companion chat robot, Smart Hanhan, at a product launch event. Priced at RMB 399 ($55), the furry toy-like device will go on sale starting Nov. 28. The robot supports human-like interaction, offering touch-based responses such as stroking and tickling triggered by touch or blowing. It can also provide daily […]
item.content:encoded: Huawei on Tuesday unveiled its first companion chat robot, Smart Hanhan, at a product launch event. Priced at RMB 399 ($55), the furry toy-like device will go on sale starting Nov. 28. The robot supports human-like interaction, offering touch-based responses such as stroking and tickling triggered by touch or blowing. It can also provide daily rhythm services, including morning greetings and bedtime good-night messages based on the user’s schedule. Smart Hanhan is customizable in that users can adjust its fur color and add accessories to create a personalized look. Powered by HarmonyOS technology, the device supports cross-device state syncing and Huawei’s “tap-to-share” feature for quick interaction between devices. [Huawei, in Chinese]

item.title: Over 5,000 Global Attendees Celebrate the Successful Debut of the XIN Summit, Showcasing the Next Generation of Innovation From the Greater Bay Area to the World
item.dc:creator: TechNode Staff
item.description: The inaugural XIN Summit concluded on 16 November with a powerful debut presented by BEYOND Expo — Asia’s largest technology innovation and ecosystem event. Focused on AI Hardware Ecosystems and Frontier Technologies, the Summit connected Media Day, the 2025 “Next Star” Global Innovation Challenge Awards Ceremony, a two-day Innovation Summit, curated Innovation Exhibition, and high-efficiency investment matchmaking to demonstrate how technology, […]
item.content:encoded:
The inaugural XIN Summit concluded on 16 November with a powerful debut presented by BEYOND Expo — Asia’s largest technology innovation and ecosystem event. Focused on AI Hardware Ecosystems and Frontier Technologies, the Summit connected Media Day, the 2025 “Next Star” Global Innovation Challenge Awards Ceremony, a two-day Innovation Summit, curated Innovation Exhibition, and high-efficiency investment matchmaking to demonstrate how technology, talent, and global capital converge in one accelerated platform.

This year’s edition brought together over 5,000 global participants, including 150+ startups, 100+ media outlets, and 100+ investment institutions, strengthening XIN’s role as a hub where deep-tech breakthroughs are showcased, scaled, and propelled toward global markets.

Media Day: Global Exposure for Rising Innovators

Held on 14 November, the XIN Summit Media Day provided exclusive early access to exhibitors, journalists, and industry opinion leaders. Selected startups engaged in substantive exchanges on commercialization strategies, research advancements, and global market positioning — building momentum before the Summit opened to the public.

Welcome Party & 2025 “Next Star” Awards: Celebrating Creative Technology and Breakthrough Talent

This year’s “Next Star” Global Innovation Challenge (Shenzhen Nanshan) selected 56 finalists from over 2,600 submissions worldwide, spanning AI, biomedicine, new materials, and advanced manufacturing. The ceremony featured an AI-powered fashion performance, interactive experiences, and the signature Fund at First Pitch (FAFP)rapid investment session. Industry leaders and technology visionaries joined to recognize the world’s most promising emerging teams.

80+ Global Leaders Share Deep Insights at the Innovation Summit

Across two days, the Innovation Summit staged 25+ keynotes, panels, and fireside conversations led by 80+ influential speakers from Greater China, Asia-Pacific, Europe, North America, and the Middle East.

Topics explored included AI breakthroughs, intelligent hardware deployment, deep-tech commercialization, and global go-to-market strategies. The program culminated in a cross-disciplinary exchange between renowned lyricist Cheuk Fai Lau and Dr. Lu Gang, Co-Founder of BEYOND Expo, reflecting how technology continues to reshape creativity and cultural communication.

Dr. Lu Gang, Co-founder of BEYOND Expo remarked: “XIN Summit stands for more than innovation, it’s a platform where global creators, founders, and investors can test ideas, accelerate commercialization, and collaborate on technologies that will redefine tomorrow’s world.”


Image: 80+ Global Leaders Share Deep Insights at the XIN Summit 2025


Image: Cross-disciplinary exchange between renowned lyricist Cheuk Fai Lau and Dr. Lu Gang, Co-Founder of BEYOND Expo

150 Companies Showcase Immersive Global Breakthroughs

The Innovation Exhibition featured 150+ companies presenting next-generation solutions in AI, embodied intelligence, smart manufacturing, health tech, sustainability, and consumer innovation. Attendees experienced tangible prototypes ranging from natural-interaction robots and advanced AIGC tools to professional health monitoring systems and low-carbon technologies. This exhibition enabled direct collaboration between product teams, industry leaders, and investors, accelerating commercialization pathways and cross-industry partnerships.


Image: Over 5,000 Global Attendees Joined XIN Summit 2025 at Nanshan, Shenzhen

Championing Gender Diversity: One-Third of XIN Summit Speakers are Female

The 2025 XIN Summit placed a strong emphasis on diversity within technology leadership, with one-third of its featured speakers represented by women across deep tech, investment, AI hardware, creative technology, and global entrepreneurship.

This milestone reflects the Summit’s belief that inclusive participation drives stronger innovation outcomes, particularly in China’s rapidly advancing technology landscape. BEYOND Expo and XIN Summit are committed to establishing industry stages where female entrepreneurs and innovators gain equal access to high-value opportunities.

Looking ahead, both BEYOND Expo and XIN Summit will deepen this commitment, continuously cultivating pathways for women in tech — not only as participants, but as innovation leaders shaping transformation at global scale.


Image: Over one-third of speakers at XIN Summit 2025 is female

High-Efficiency Capital Engagement Through Targeted Investment Matchmaking

To drive real outcomes for deep-tech ventures, XIN Summit curated FAFP rapid pitching and one-on-one investor meetings, gathering over 100 active investment institutions across AI, emerging hardware, industrial technology, and deep tech. Five selected FAFP finalists — Aquarius Tech, Sengine AI, BIAI, Fluorion, and Yuanmeng Interstellar — showcased business models and proprietary technologies in a fast-paced format that delivered instant investor feedback and opportunity alignment.

Image: FAFP pitching sessions at XIN Summit 2025 bridge capital with innovation

XIN Summit: A New Starting Point for Global Innovation Collaboration

The successful debut of XIN Summit affirms its role as a new bridge connecting China’s innovation ecosystem with global markets. Built upon the momentum of the Greater Bay Area, XIN is designed to accelerate emerging ventures, amplify global market access, and foster partnerships that enable deep-tech commercialization at scale.

As the Summit concludes its inaugural edition, it marks the beginning of a long-term platform where technology breakthroughs, cross-border collaboration, and next-generation entrepreneurship converge. From Shenzhen to the world, the XIN Summit will continue to champion innovators building the future.

Stay connected for more highlights and updates ahead of the next edition.

item.title: Global Top Startup Teams Shine as XIN Summit Ignites the Night with the Next Star Awards
item.dc:creator: TechNode Staff
item.description: On the eve of the XIN Summit (powered by BEYOND), two major pre-summit programs — XIN Summit Media Day and the Entrepreneurs Next Star & Million Prize Global Challenge 2025 Awards Ceremony — set the stage for a landmark gathering of global deep-tech innovators. Held on November 14, these flagship events built strong early momentum […]
item.content:encoded:
On the eve of the XIN Summit (powered by BEYOND), two major pre-summit programs — XIN Summit Media Day and the Entrepreneurs Next Star & Million Prize Global Challenge 2025 Awards Ceremony — set the stage for a landmark gathering of global deep-tech innovators. Held on November 14, these flagship events built strong early momentum for the Summit and delivered its first defining highlight: a celebration of world-class entrepreneurship and breakthrough innovation emerging from Shenzhen Nanshan.


Image: XIN Summit, powered by BEYOND, was held at Shenzhen Nansha from November 15-16, 2025

As one of China’s most influential innovation and entrepreneurship programs, the “Next Star” Global Challenge has become a signature platform rooted in Nanshan, connected nationwide, and radiating globally. This year, more than 2,600 projects participated across sectors including artificial intelligence, biomedicine, new materials, and advanced equipment manufacturing. After a rigorous global selection process, 56 standout teams advanced to the Global Finals, reaffirming Nanshan’s position as a global magnet for science and technology talent and a driver of future industry development.

When night fell, the atmosphere reached its peak with a dynamic Welcome Party and Awards Ceremony. While Media Day emphasized expert exchange and global communication, the evening program adopted an open, creative, and celebratory format — showcasing a fusion of technology, culture, and global innovation.

Highlights of the Awards Ceremony

The ceremony opened with an AI-driven fashion showcase that blended algorithmic creativity with artistic expression, demonstrating the increasingly porous boundaries between design, technology, and digital culture. The program continued with an unconventional twist as a stand-up comedian hosted the night, weaving light-hearted reflections on technology and entrepreneurship into an evening that balanced ceremony with humor and inspiration.

Throughout the event, outstanding startup teams from China and around the world took center stage. Finalists from Europe, North America, the Asia-Pacific region, and across China were honored across multiple tracks, culminating in first, second, and third prizes in both the Team and Enterprise tracks. Their achievements reflect Shenzhen Nanshan’s evolution into a global destination for startup acceleration and commercialization, strengthening its reputation as a leading deep-tech hub.

Awards were presented by distinguished guests from fields such as advanced engineering, micro-reaction science, global professional networks, and venture investment. Their participation underscored the competition’s influence and its role in accelerating promising scientific and technological breakthroughs toward real-world application.

Image: AI Fashion Show by LookAI at XIN Summit Welcome Party

Fund at First Pitch: Innovation Happens in Real Time

A special highlight of the evening was the “Fund at First Pitch” rapid-fire pitching session. In this high-intensity segment, founders were challenged to articulate the core value of their technology within moments, receiving on-the-spot evaluations from a panel of experts spanning scientific research, investment, and global industry insights. The immediate, candid feedback mirrored the pace of real-world innovation and gave entrepreneurs an opportunity to refine their positioning in front of seasoned evaluators.

The session was supported by key investment partners, further reinforcing the competition’s mission to drive tangible outcomes for startups by connecting them with capital, mentorship, and industrial resources. Following the pitches, the evening transitioned into improvisational performances and open networking, where participants engaged in cross-disciplinary exchange — expanding conversations from technical roadmaps to creative expression, industry culture, and long-term collaboration.

Image: Fund-at-First-Pitch rapid-fire pitching session at XIN Summit Welcome Party

The two-day Summit will bring together global entrepreneurs, investors, and technology leaders for high-level forums, an innovation exhibition, and curated investment matchmaking programs. As a new sub-brand created by BEYOND Expo, the XIN Summit aims to redefine the landscape of deep-tech collaboration in China. Anchored in the industrial strength of the Greater Bay Area, the Summit serves as a gateway connecting Chinese innovation with global resources—accelerating breakthrough applications across intelligent systems, smart hardware, and next-generation technological infrastructure.

Additional information, including keynote speakers and the full agenda, can be found on the official website: www.xinsummit.com

item.title: Singapore’s national AI program drops Meta model and switches to Alibaba’s Qwen
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Singapore’s national AI program has moved its Sea-Lion large language model off Meta’s model family and adopted Alibaba Cloud’s Qwen architecture, according to information cited by foreign media from AI Singapore (AISG). The latest version, Qwen-Sea-Lion-v4, was trained with technical support from Alibaba Cloud and built on the Qwen3-32B foundation model, which covers 119 languages […]
item.content:encoded:
Singapore’s national AI program has moved its Sea-Lion large language model off Meta’s model family and adopted Alibaba Cloud’s Qwen architecture, according to information cited by foreign media from AI Singapore (AISG). The latest version, Qwen-Sea-Lion-v4, was trained with technical support from Alibaba Cloud and built on the Qwen3-32B foundation model, which covers 119 languages and dialects and was trained on 36 trillion tokens. Alibaba Cloud said the model received an additional 100 billion Southeast Asian language tokens for this collaboration, while AISG contributed regional datasets and handled evaluation.

Qwen-Sea-Lion-v4 currently ranks first among open-source models under 200 billion parameters in the “South-east Asian Holistic Evaluation of Language Models.” The model is available on the AI Singapore website and Hugging Face. Singapore launched its national multimodal model program in December 2023 with SGD 70 million (USD 51 million) in funding. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: TSMC to expand 2nm capacity to 10 fabs, adding three in China’s Taiwan
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: TSMC is reportedly planning to increase its number of 2-nanometre advanced process wafer fabs in Taiwan from seven to ten. The three new fabs are expected to be located in Tainan, complementing the existing 2nm production in Hsinchu (two fabs) and Kaohsiung (five fabs). Each new fab is estimated to cost about NT$300 billion ($9.3 billion), bringing […]
item.content:encoded: TSMC is reportedly planning to increase its number of 2-nanometre advanced process wafer fabs in Taiwan from seven to ten. The three new fabs are expected to be located in Tainan, complementing the existing 2nm production in Hsinchu (two fabs) and Kaohsiung (five fabs). Each new fab is estimated to cost about NT$300 billion ($9.3 billion), bringing the total investment for the three sites to NT$900 billion ($28 billion). The expansion aims to meet surging demand for AI chips and high-performance computing. TSMC expects the new fabs to begin mass production by 2026, with a combined monthly output exceeding 100,000 wafers. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: NetEase’s open-world wuxia game Where Winds Meet hits 250,000 concurrent players on Steam
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China’s NetEase has seen its open-world martial-arts title Where Winds Meet gain strong momentum on Steam since its global launch on Nov. 15. The game’s concurrent player count on the platform hit 251,000 on Nov. 24, while total players surpassed two million in the first week, placing it among Steam’s top five best-sellers. The game […]
item.content:encoded: China’s NetEase has seen its open-world martial-arts title Where Winds Meet gain strong momentum on Steam since its global launch on Nov. 15. The game’s concurrent player count on the platform hit 251,000 on Nov. 24, while total players surpassed two million in the first week, placing it among Steam’s top five best-sellers. The game is an open-world wuxia game that emphasizes free exploration and realistic action combat. Players can traverse the large map using light-step movement and stealth, experiencing a high degree of freedom in exploration. Players can traverse terrain with light-step movement, engage in stealth, and craft items through gathering, cooking, and other life skills. Story quests, side missions, and diverse martial-arts styles combine to create an immersive wuxia experience. [Steam, in Chinese]
